R/O was able to talk to the juveniles and asked them to step outside with their hands up, while detained outside, the mother gave permission to check the unit - no weapons or other suspects in the residence.  The victim stated the subjects were not the offenders in the robbery.  The subjects were released and given stop cards. FI and pedestrian stop data sheets were completed.

R/O responded to the area for an armed robbery call.  The victim stated the offenders ran into a location after the robbery, R/O spoke to one of the residents of the building, made contact with unit 1, she stated she is at work and her sons were home, she put them on a 3way call.  R/O was able to talk to the juveniles and asked them to step outside with their hands up, while detained outside, the mother gave permission to check the unit - no weapons or other suspects in the residence.  The victim stated the subjects were not the offenders in the robbery.  The subjects were released and given stop cards. FI and pedestrian stop data sheets were completed.
Theft subject was exiting the alley on a bike during a call of a suspicious person trying door handles.  The subject stated he was headed to work and rides his bike there everyday. Nothing further.
The subject was stopped and positively ID'd as the offender who removed a PRIDE flag, No complaints to be signed, R/O released the subject from the scene.
The subject was arrested for attempting to steal a motor vehicle.
The subject was arrested after being identified as the offender in a domestic battery.

R/O responded to caller stating a suspicious person hovering around the garage. R/O observe the subject on  a bike and failed to stop a the stop sign.  Another officer translated for R/O, the subject admitted to being in the alley and stated he was smoking.  A LEADS check came back clear and he was given a warning to stop at stop signs and released from the scene after being provided a stop card.
The subject matched the description of an assault offender. After a brief foot chase, he was stopped/detained. He was positively ID'd and placed into custody, and TOT the station for processing.

R/O dispatched for a welfare check to a subject who was yelling at people in the street.  A witness stated they were approached by the subject in an aggressive manner, carrying a pipe and saying threatening words.  Another witness stated the subject approached him in a threatening manner - both did not want to sign complaints but wanted OPPD to provide mental health service to the subject.  The subject was released from the scene and left the area.

R/O responded to the area for a suspicious person call, the subject was pushing a garbage can to the callers' window.  R/O observed a subject matching the description given out, pulling a blue refuse cart and a wagon loaded with miscellaneous items.  The subject stated he was collecting items from the alleys and dumpsters. R/O observed the refuse cart to be marked with 414 Elgin Ave - that is from Forest PK.  FPPD checked that location and related no police service was required.  The subject checked clear through LEADS, he was supplied a pedestrian stop receipt and released from the scene.

R/O responded to a suspicious person call, the subject was looking at bike locks with a flashlight.  R/O made contact with the subject, who was on a bike. The subject was clear LEADS, the bike as well.  The subject stated he was dropped off in the area and was riding to a residence on Austin Blvd.

R/O responded to a retail theft call and observed the subject to fit the description. R/O detained the subject and recovered the proceeds, the items were returned to the business.  Caller refused to sign complaints - the suspect was released at the scene and provided with a stop card.

R/O responded  to the area for a suspicious male walking around a camper in the alley.  The subject stated he was just walking in the alley and was on his way to his mother's home, who lived on Marion St. The subject denied all medical services.

R/O responded to a retail theft call, the officers observed the subject was stopped at an intersection close to the store, a show up was done and positive ID was established. The store did not want to sign complaints, and the subject was released on scene.

In Summary: On 01 July 25 at 0023hrs R/O observed a subject a riding a bicycle with no front/rear headlamps W/B on Madison St approaching Highland Ave. R/O placed a stopped on the subject at Highland and Madison due to him violating L.O. 15-2-11 (Lighted lamps and audible signaling devices on bicycle). R/O ran the name through Leads which came back clear. R/O also advised that he is required to have a front/rear headlamp on a bicycle while being operated at nighttime. R/O gave  a verbal warning.R/O`s BWC was activated throughout this incident.

In summary: R/O responded to the area of Jackson/Ridgeland reference man with a gun (m/w blond hair, black shirt black shorts with a possible rifle in book bag). Upon arrival R/O observed a subject, matching the description, on a bike s/b Cuyler from Jackson with a rifle butt stock sticking out of his book bag. R/O stopped the subject,  at Highland/Harrison St.R/Os determined the rifle in the book bag was a BB gun. R/Os contacted the mother, who responded to the scene. The BB gun was turned over to the mother to be transported in her vehicle and she was advised of the reason for the stop.

In summary: R/o was monitoring radio dispatch via WSCDC relating that an offender (M/B, wearing a yellow vest, holding a khahki green bag and riding a bike) removed a rug from 4XX Madison and fled the area E/b on Madison.R/o observed the subject that matched the description (M/b, wearing a yellow vest, holding a khahki green bag and riding a bike) at approx 59XX W Madison St. shortly after. R/o subsequently stopped the subject, who was in possession of the stolen item (rug). The subject allowed R/O to search his green duffel bag for other possible stolen items. R/O did not locate any additional possible stolen items, however R/O also observed two power sanders (unknown owner information) inthe subjects possession that he related were not his.  R/o was then told that the victim did not want to sign complaints but rather wanted her property returned and a report generated.  R/O TOT the sanders to Ofc Kurasz #401 who then inventoried the items into the BEAST for proof of ownership.
R/Oalso observed the subject to be a suspect in an additional burglary case that required him to be identified. R/O forwarded the information to Ofc Kurasz #401 who took the initial report. The subject was released from the scene.BWC available.

In summary: R/o was dispatched to the area of Lake/Ridgeland in reference to a bike theft.  R/o was monitoring radio dispatch via WSCDC who related that the offenders (2 M/b`s approx 14 yoa, with short dreads wearing a white t-shirt and sweat pants) removed an orange BMX bicycle and were last seen riding away N/b on Ridgeland from Lake. Upon arrival into the area R/o observed two M/B`s young, both with short dreads, wearing sweat shirts (one grey, one black) with white t-shirts underneath and sweat pants walking W/b on Superior from Elmwood. R/o also observed the two M/B`s to be out of breath and sweating profusely.R/O subsequently stopped both subjects at Elmwood/Superior and identified one subject who  related that he did not remove a bicycle from Ridgeland/Lake.R/O subsequently then located the Orange Bmx bicycle in the rear of 3XX N Ridgeland Ave which was positively identified by the victim as his bike.   The victim negatively identifiedthe subject  as being involved.
R/o spoke with the subject's  mother  via phone who was informed of the situation but was out of town and could not retrieve her son. She related that she wanted R/O to release the subject on the street. See additional FI for other subject.....
BWC available.

R/S responded for a retail theft at 4XX Madison where the caller said 2 M/Bs got in a vehicle. R/S observed the vehicle with given IL Ref leaving the lot and conducted a traffic stop on it at 1XX Madison. R/S spoke to driver and the passenger, who related they did come from 4XX Madison St but did not steal anything. OPPD made contact with the caller who confirmed they did not take anything but that they did get in a verbal altercation with employees. Both subjects were released on scene.

In summary, while on routine patrol, R/O placed a stop on an individual that fit the description of a person that was seen burglarizing an auto at  4XX Chestnut Ln on 13-Jul-2025 at 2325 hours and was described as a male/black teenager, with short hair, riding a blue Divvy bicycle and last seen traveling northbound from that location. The individual was also seen by R/O in the area of North Blvd/Oak Park Ave at the time when a call came out about a burglary to auto at 7XX North Blvd on 07-Jul-2025 at 00:25 hours. While driving northbound on S Ridgeland Ave, R/O viewed the individual riding a blue Divvy bicycle southbound on S Ridgeland Ave, at Washington Blvd. R/O made a U-turn and then viewed the individual make a left turn and travel eastbound on Madison Ave. R/O conducted a street stop based on reasonable suspicion. R/O viewed the individual`s bike to not have the required color reflectors on the wheels, which is a violation of Local Ordinance Code 15-2-11(C): Lighted Lamps and Audible signal on Bike.When asked for identification by R/O, the individual related that he did not have a driver`s license or state identification. While on scene, R/O was unable to find local contact information and LEADS name check came back with negative results.
The individual, was taken into custody and transported to the Oak Park Police Station. The individual was given a Municipal Code Violation Citation ticket  and was released from the station at 0250 hours on 14-Jul-2025.
BWC activated but not viewed for this report.

R/O conducted a street stop on the juveniles who related to R/O they are 15 years old and were walking home to 535 N Central Ave. Chicago, IL.R/O was able to contact the mother who related she is the mother and aunt. R/O gave a verbal warning for violating curfew. Mother/aunt came and picked them up at approx. 0232hrs.R/O completed IDOT pedestrain stop sheets.BWC was activated during the time of the stop

In summary: On 15-JUL-25 at approx. 0212hrs R/O was on patrol at Taylor/ Ontario when R/O observed two juveniles.

R/O was patrolling E/B on North Blvd towards Forest Ave due to knowledge of reported burglaries and thefts that have been reported in that area. R/O observed an unknown subject tampering with a bicycle that was secured to a bike rack using a large metal object. R/O conducted an investigative stop on the subject, he was observed holding the metal object, now identified as a door hinge, which he used to defeat the cable lock on the bike. He related that he was taking the bike to go meet with someone and was trying to get there in a hurry. The subject was detained at 0228 hrs. R/O conducted a LEADS check that  showed a issued warrant out of Cook County. WSCDC confirmed the Warrant at 0259 hrs which revealed he had 2 active warrants for FTA (Failure to Appear) for retail thefts. No victim was found for the missing bicycles.

See offense report for further.

In summary: On 16JUL25 at approximately 0346hrs R/O responded to a WSCDC dispatch of a meet complainant located in the north parking lot of 3 Erie Ct. Reporting party  related a M/B  with dreadlocks, sitting inside a silver Jeep bearing  stuck his head toward a partially rolled down window and related "Bitch I`ll shoot you." caller  related she did not see a firearm.The boyfriend related he observed the subject exit the vehicle with what appeared to be a black firearm, that was bigger than a pistol and stated "What the fuck you doing bro." The subject  then entered the JEEP.
 a f/w stuck her hand inside his partially rolled down window of his JEEP, asked if you`re okay. Teh subject related he was startled and stated "get the fuck away from my car."
The subject  related he does not have a firearm in his possession and never exited the vehicle.No weapon was recovered.The subject  consented to a vehicle search (signed).He was given a stop card.

R/O responded to 4XX Madison St for Remove Unwanted/Criminal Trespass. Brown was provided a trespass warning and notified that he was not allowed on Jewel (4XX Madison St) property. Brown violated the warning and was placed into custody.

In summary, on 17 JUL 25 at 1602 hrs, R/O responded to 10 Pleasant St for a suspicious person. Per the caller (refused) a subject (M/B, 25-30 yoa, thin, red shirt, black pants) was pacing outside with a large butcher knife. R/O responded to the area and observed a subject in the rear of 2XX of S. Humphrey alley bending over by the garbage cans. R/O exited the vehicle and approached the subject. R/O observed a large knife sheath attache to his right leg. R/O observed a large knife with an approx. 12 inch blade by the garbage can where R/O had initially observed the subject  bent over. He  related that he was in the alley doing "community service." R/O observed a large cart that belonged to the subject with various items in it, indicating that he was junking in the alley.R/O explained to the subject  that it is unlawful for him to openly carry a a fixed blade knife longer than 3 inches. R/O collected the knife. Knife was inventoried for safe-keeping a OPPD.   R/O`s BWC was activated.

Witness related that he observed the subject (M/B or H, skinny, 508-509, dark complexion, beard, 25 yoa, wearing a red shirt, stripped gym shorts and baklava) in the rear  damaging a Hyundai with a rock. Witness related that he originally had observed the subject in front of his house  just prior to the incident the subject was inside Carnival Food Store. 
Witness related that the last known location for the subject was Fox Park.Ofc. Hernandez observed a subject (M/H, shirtless, jeans) at Fox park during the call for service but this observation was prior to witness relating that the subject had changed clothes and was currently wearing jeans and was shirtless.
An assist FD call was dispatched for 7XX Madison St. The subject  was sleeping in the parking lot. Subject was wearing jeans and was shirtless. Ofc. Hernandez related that the subject matched description of the subject he observed in the park.Witness related that he was unable to come to the location and identify the subject at this time. R/O unable to reach victims.

While conducting traffic mission control on Lombard/ Harvard, R/O observed the subject  driving N/B bound on Lombard disregarding the stop sign at Lombard/Harvard. R/O conducted a traffic stop and issued citations for disregarding stop sign and driving with expired license.

SUMMARY: R/S responded to a retail theft at 4XX Madison where it was broadcasted that two offenders were entering a vehicle and leaving the scene. As R/S arrived, multiple loss prevention agents were at the front entrance and pointed to a GRY SUV (now known to be a 2001 Chevy,  that was leaving the lot to go E/B through the north Madison alley. R/S got behind the vehicle and conducted a traffic stop at 4XX Ridgeland. Officers spoke to loss prevention who related that no items were taken at this time and they wanted the driver and passenger trespassed. During the stop a LEADS check revealed the subject to have a suspended license and there was no proof of insurance for the vehicle. The subject was taken into custody, cited, and then NTA from scene. The vehicle was towed by O`Hare for lack of a license and no insurance.

In Summary: On 23-JUL-2025, 0121hrs, R/O responded to the area of Oak Park Ave/Randolph St in reference to a person with a gun call that originated at Harlem Ave/Randolph St. Dispatch advised the caller stated a subject in a black Cadillac pointed a gun at them following a road rage incident. The caller advised that the vehicle was last seen E/B Randolph St. 
R/O observed a black Chrysler 300  traveling N/B Oak Park Ave from Washington Blvd and observed at least 3 occupants in the vehicle (ultimately found to be 4). 
R/O conducted an investigatory stop of the vehicle at 124 N Oak Park Ave. All occupants were ordered to exit with which they complied. The occupants were identified, a search of the vehicle revealed a loaded 9mm Glock 19X handgun in the glovebox. Further investigation revealed thata subject was the offender who possessed and brandished the handgun during the road rage incident. He was placed into custody for AGG UUW. (See arrest report for further). All other occupants and the vehicle were released on scene. BWC was activated.

R/O observed one subject to be in possession of 1 bag of marijuana. R/O asked another subject what was in his backpack to which he replied "ammo". R/O searched the backpack and observed 6 rounds of ammunition (Winchester 12 gauge x1, 38 special x2, Winchester 380 auto x1, R-P 40 Smith & Wesson x2). At approx. 2049hrs The subject was detained.  A LEADS check  revealed the subject  to not have a valid FOID or CCL. At approx. 2050hrs the subject was placed into custody (see arrest card for further). 
 R/O confiscated the marijuana and ET  inventoried it. ET inventoried the ammunition.
R/O`s were able to make contact with the other subjects  parents to notfiy them of the stop. 
R/O filled out IDOT pedestrian stop sheets.
BWC was activated during the time of the call.

In summary: R/O initiated a traffic stop on a vehicle  at Austin/Erie.  Upon passenger side approach R/O was met by driver, who provided R/O with his drivers license and insurance.  R/O observed a single passenger in the rear passenger seat of the vehicle whom POTTER related was his cousin.  R/O immediately observed suspect cannabis residue on the center console of the vehicle.  R/O questioned the passenger how old he was to which he responded he was seventeen (17) and related he did not have an ID on him.  R/O requested both occupants to exit the vehicle and both complied without incident.  Once out of the vehicle R/O requested the name and date of birth of the passenger and he provided a name and date of birth.  Based on the information provided R/O detained the subject with the use of handcuffs and placed him into the rear of squad 528 for further investigation due to his date of birth and age not matching.  The other subject  related to R/O that his cousin lied about his name and date of birth likely due to a warrant that was just taken care of at court.  
R/O requested the subject to  provide his real name and date of birth again to which he provided   A LEADS check revealed the subject to be clear.   
Both subjects were released from the scene with a warning.

In summary: On 09-OCT-25 at approx. 2139hrs R/O was responding to a burglary that had just occurred at 6 Thomas St. 
Before arrival, WESTCOM (dispatch) related the caller observed a cut lock to a storage unit and an unknown subject in the basement where the storage units are located. The caller described the offender as a male black with a neon yellow vest and a garbage bag on a bicycle.  R/O arrived on scene and R/O was flagged down by the witness,who related the offender was last seen in the Austin/ Humphrey alley southbound. R/O then went south to look to see if the offender was in the alley. R/O observed a subject matching the description as the offender (Male, black, yellow neon vest, riding a bicycle, carrying bags). In summary: R/O initiated a traffic stop on a vehicle  at Austin/Harrison.  Upon passenger side approach R/O was met by driver and front seat passenger  who both provided R/O with identification. In summary: On 18-OCT-2025 at 0011 hrs, R/O observed a vehicle  traveling W/B Jackson Blvd from Ridgeland Ave at a high rate of speed. The vehicle disregarded the stop sign at East Ave/Jackson and turned N/B onto East Ave at which point in time the driver turned off his lights and blacked out before turning them back on. R/O initiated a stop on the vehicle at East Ave/Adams and made contact with the driver, who provided R/O with his IL identification card, the subject advised R/O that the vehicle belonged to his mother and that he was in the process of dropping off an Uber eats order at an unknown address. While speaking to the subject, R/O smelled a strong odor of burnt and fresh cannabis emitting from inside the vehicle. While standing at the front passenger side door of the vehicle, observed in plain view a small Black rifle with a Grey magazine described as a "Draco" partially stuffed underneath the front passenger seat. Ofc questioned the subject about the rifle at which point in time he locked the doors, rolled up the windows and fled at a high rate of speed. He fled N/B East Ave disregarding the traffic control device at Madison Street and continued N/B where he disregarded the traffic control device at East Ave/Washington Blvd before making an E/B turn onto Washington Blvd. R/O was unable to locate the subject after he made the E/B turn onto Washington Blvd. R/O inventoriedthe subjects  IL identification card into the BEAST.

The subject related to R/O that he doesn`t possess a valid FOID/CCL card.
R/O transported him back to the station to be processed. 
Once inside the station, the subject was foubd to have an active warrant out of cook county for shoplifting. 
E.T Nash fielded tested the substance which provided a positive result 0.7 grams for positive suspected heroin.  The subject was charged with an L.O. for no Bicycle light, Obstruction of Identification, Possession of Ammunition without a FOID or CCl and also have an active warrant. Subject was also charged with possession of controlled substance. The above bullets were inventoried into Beast.  
R/O`s BWC was activated throughout this incident.

On 14 NOV 25 at 1117 hours, R/O responded to (Senator Don Harmon Office) reference an aggravated assault, later reclassified as an armed robbery.Upon arrival, dispatch related the suspect came into the office with a knife and took one of the employees phone. The suspect was described as m/w, 40s, 6`0 ft, thin build, bulky green coat and blue pants. OFC  (primary officer) related the phone was pinging on the 6600 block of Bloomingdale Ave, traveling e/b Bloomingdale towards Narragansett. R/O responded to the area of Bloomingdale Ave and Narragansett and observed a subject matching the description standing alone on the corner of Bloomingdale Ave and Narragansett. Prior to making contact with the subject, R/O asked dispatch for the description of the subject once again, as well as an updated location of the victims phone ping. OFC  then related the phone was pinging at Bloomingdale and Narragansett.
R/O then conducted a terry stop on the suspect  at Bloomingdale and Narragansett (R/O did not observe any other civilians or suspects walking in the area). OFC  then ordered the subject to get on the ground. R/O detained subject at 1134 hours without further incident. The subject was asked "where is the knife" and he  stated it was in one of his pockets. R/O was able to recover a knife in his pocket and also located a phone, that had a missed call from the caller, who was the original 911 caller, who was on scene with the victim and was attempting to call the victims phone).  OFC transported the victim to the scene and conducted a show up on the suspect, who was positively identified as the offender at 1142hrs by the victim. The victim also positively identified her cell phone and the knife that was used in the commission of the crime at 1143hrs. The subject  was then placed into custody at 1145 hours (handcuffed, double locked and check for fit) and transported to the station in squad 527 for processing. 
At the station the subject was read his Miranda rights at 1210hrs in the presence of OFC. The subject refused to make any statements and requested a lawyer. ET Rumoro took pictures of the scene and inventoried the knife as evidence.He was fed and was allowed to make phone calls. He made a phone call at 1620 hours.ASA approved felony charges for armed robbery on 15Nov25 at 1114hrs. BWC was activated but not reviewed prior to the completion of this report.

On 23 DEC 25 at approximately 0139 hours, while conducting a traffic stop, the reporting officer (R/O) observed a green leafy substance consistent with marijuana located in the cup holder and center console area of the vehicle.

On 27-DEC-2025 at 0026 Hrs, R/O conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle (Silver Infinti G37 which disregarded the traffic control signal while traveling N/B Austin Blvd at Augusta. R/O curbed the vehicle on the 5900 block of Division in Chicago. While making a passenger side approach on the vehicle, R/O smelled a strong odor of burnt cannabis emitting from the vehicle. R/O made contact with the driver  who produced his drivers license and insurance card. While speaking with the subject, R/O observed in plain view a green leaf like substance suspect cannabis on the passenger floor board. A/O MEJIA who was standing on the driver side also located in plain view a green leaf like substance suspect cannabis on the driver side floor board. LEE denied having any cannabis in the vehicle nor smoking in the vehicle prior to the stop. R/O informed LEE that a search of the vehicle would be conducted due to the cannabis in plain view and he and his passenger were ordered to exit. (unknown name) LEE became non compliant and advised officers that he was not going to exit the vehicle. LEE then locked the doors, looked down at his gear shifter (still in drive) and attempted to roll up his windows making R/O believe he was going to flee. R/O then stopped the passenger side window from rolling up, reach inside of the vehicle to unlock the doors and placed the vehicle in park. LEE was then removed and a search was conducted. Located inside the vehicle was a small non criminal amount of cannabis residue on the floor board of the vehicle in the front and back seat area. Also located was a small zip lock baggie in the driver door panel with small residue of suspect cannabis. LEE was ultimately issued a citation for the red light violation and requested a sergeant. Sgt NG responded to the scene and spoke with LEE. Upon leaving the scene, LEE sped off E/B Division St a very high rate of speed causing his wheels to squeal excessively which was captured on R/O`s squad camera.
